## Decisions

### DEC-001: Start a fresh Kiroku session for the current conceptual work instead of prete...
2026-04-19T19:31:44+02:00 - [delegability-core-thesis](sessions/2026-04-19-delegability-core-thesis.md#L30)
Rationale: Observable Autonomy depends on truthful trail boundaries. A clean new session is more honest than retroactively smuggling new reasoning into an already closed session.
Alternatives: append to the closed `skills-v2-cross-validation` session (blurs two different tasks), leave the discussion uncaptured (violates the intent of Kiroku)
### DEC-002: Frame the possible future "AI protocol" first as a delegability contract, not...
2026-04-19T19:31:44+02:00 - [delegability-core-thesis](sessions/2026-04-19-delegability-core-thesis.md#L44)
Rationale: The unresolved problem is the contract for authority, evidence, challenge, and revocation. Wire format and interoperability standards come later, after the minimum trust contract is stable.
Alternatives: jump directly to a protocol analogy like HTTP or sockets (premature formalization), avoid protocol thinking entirely (misses that the work may eventually need a standard interface)
### DEC-003: Produce two stabilizing artifacts: a one-page plain-language thesis and a fir...
2026-04-19T19:31:44+02:00 - [delegability-core-thesis](sessions/2026-04-19-delegability-core-thesis.md#L54)
Rationale: The user's memory problem is part of the problem domain itself. If the framework cannot be re-explained simply and revisited later without re-deriving it from scratch, it is not yet a trustworthy adoption architecture.
Alternatives: continue only in chat (too ephemeral), fold the explanation into `PRINCIPLES.md` directly (mixes theory with onboarding), postpone until protocol design is mature (delays needed clarity)
### DEC-004: After documenting the transformation clearly, the next best move is externali...
2026-04-19T19:31:44+02:00 - [delegability-core-thesis](sessions/2026-04-19-delegability-core-thesis.md#L70)
Rationale: Self-runs are still useful, but at this moment they risk returning to self-referential improvement. The stronger test is whether the new plain-language thesis and delegability contract help the suite perform on an external target or help a fresh observer understand and challenge it.
Alternatives: immediately run another full Kata cycle on the suite (likely low marginal insight), stop after documentation only (misses external proof), jump straight to protocol formalization (too early)
### DEC-005: Use the first external target run as the concrete worked example rather than ...
2026-04-19T19:31:44+02:00 - [delegability-core-thesis](sessions/2026-04-19-delegability-core-thesis.md#L80)
Rationale: A real target exercises the contract under actual constraints and produces evidence that is more valuable than a fabricated illustration.
Alternatives: write a hypothetical example first (clearer but less probative), postpone the example until later (keeps the comprehension gap open)
### DEC-006: Use `c:\git\datakit` as the first external target for the clarified suite.
2026-04-19T19:31:44+02:00 - [delegability-core-thesis](sessions/2026-04-19-delegability-core-thesis.md#L86)
Rationale: It is small enough to complete in one session, has an existing test suite, and its purpose (data validation and transformation utilities) is concrete enough to make the trust/evidence framing legible without requiring domain-specific institutional complexity.
Alternatives: `mathkit` (also viable, but slightly broader), a larger external target (more realistic but too large for the first worked example)
### DEC-007: Kiroku tooling is PowerShell scripts + agent protocol, not a VS Code extensio...
2026-04-19T17:10:16+02:00 - [rebuild-planning](sessions/2026-04-19-rebuild-planning.md#L40)
Rationale: Target environment is Windows with PowerShell 5.1. No dependency installation. Scripts handle mechanical validation; the agent handles capture. The agent IS the primary tool - scripts support it.
Alternatives: VS Code extension (requires extension development, violates INTENT constraint), Python CLI (requires venv management), manual-only convention (current state, proven insufficient)
### DEC-008: Split measurement into two tiers: Skill Quality (input) and Work Quality (out...
2026-04-19T17:10:16+02:00 - [rebuild-planning](sessions/2026-04-19-rebuild-planning.md#L56)
Rationale: You need both. Input quality (are the skills well-written?) prevents garbage-in. Output quality (does work done under these skills actually succeed?) prevents vanity metrics. Neither is sufficient alone. The current system has only input metrics.
Alternatives: Replace rubric v3 entirely (loses 46 runs of calibration data), keep rubric v3 only (perpetuates the blind spot), merge into one rubric (conflates different measurement targets)
### DEC-009: Use pass/fail for Tier 2 (output metrics), not 1-10 scales.
2026-04-19T17:10:16+02:00 - [rebuild-planning](sessions/2026-04-19-rebuild-planning.md#L66)
Rationale: The v1/v2 rubric proved that 1-10 scales invite ad-hoc scoring that converges to the ceiling without meaning. Pass/fail forces binary evidence: the work succeeded or it didn't.
Alternatives: 1-10 scale (same failure mode as v1/v2), 3-point scale (marginal improvement over binary, adds complexity without clarity)
### DEC-010: The system determines what skills exist, not the human. The rebuild starts fr...
2026-04-19T17:10:16+02:00 - [rebuild-planning](sessions/2026-04-19-rebuild-planning.md#L76)
Rationale: The current 8-skill structure was invented by one model in one session. The number and nature of skills should be derived from what the Principles require, not from what happened to be created on day one.
Alternatives: Prescribe the same 8 skills with cleaner content (perpetuates the structure problem), let the human specify which skills (violates Commander's Intent)
### DEC-011: No human edits to skill files. All changes through Intents.
2026-04-19T17:10:16+02:00 - [rebuild-planning](sessions/2026-04-19-rebuild-planning.md#L80)
Rationale: This makes the audit trail honest - every change has a traceable origin. It also enforces Commander's Intent: the human defines what, the system determines how.
Alternatives: Human edits with trail documentation (partial audit trail, mixed authorship), collaborative editing (muddies the P1 test)
### DEC-012: Existing v1.34.0 is reference material, not a template. No copy-paste.
2026-04-19T17:10:16+02:00 - [rebuild-planning](sessions/2026-04-19-rebuild-planning.md#L84)
Rationale: The goal is to test whether fresh reasoning from Principles produces the same, different, or better skills than 44 runs of iterative patching. Copy-pasting would defeat the experiment.
Alternatives: Start from v1.34.0 and simplify (incremental, not a rebuild), ignore v1.34.0 entirely (loses lessons learned)
### DEC-013: The diagnostic lenses (unevenness, overburden, waste) become vocabulary withi...
2026-04-19T17:42:43+02:00 - [skills-rebuild](sessions/2026-04-19-skills-rebuild.md#L62)
Rationale: Principle 1 says "provide vocabulary, not answers." Mura/Muri/Muda are vocabulary for diagnosis. Making them separate skills fragments a single activity (diagnosis) into three prescribed steps. A competent agent should determine which lens applies when, not be told "now run Mura, now run Muri, now run Muda."
Alternatives: Keep as separate skills (v1.34.0 approach - 44 runs of this proved they're always run together under Kata anyway), merge into two skills (diagnosis + treatment - still fragments unnecessarily)
### DEC-014: Five skills, not eight. Mura, Muri, and Muda are absorbed into Kaizen as diag...
2026-04-19T17:42:43+02:00 - [skills-rebuild](sessions/2026-04-19-skills-rebuild.md#L76)
Rationale: The three standalone diagnostic skills were always invoked through Kata's orchestration, never independently. They're thinking tools, not workflows. Absorbing them into Kaizen makes the agent determine which diagnostic lens to apply based on what it encounters - exactly what P1 requires. Project-increment handles version bumps; it doesn't embody any Principle and doesn't require autonomous reasoning.
Alternatives: Keep 8 skills (perpetuates prescriptive fragmentation), keep 6 (Mura+Muri+Muda merged into one diagnostic skill separate from Kaizen - still separates diagnosis from treatment unnecessarily), keep 3 (Kaizen+Kata+Hansei - loses Kaikaku and Shiken which serve distinct purposes that can't be absorbed)
### DEC-015: Diagnostic vocabulary is embedded in Kaizen, not referenced as external skills.
2026-04-19T17:42:43+02:00 - [skills-rebuild](sessions/2026-04-19-skills-rebuild.md#L97)
Rationale: When writing Kaizen, the diagnostic lenses emerged as natural sections within the Diagnose phase. Unevenness, overburden, and waste are described as "thinking tools, not a procedure." This is the Commander's Intent test: a competent agent reading Kaizen would know to look for inconsistency, overload, and dead weight without being told to "invoke Mura." The vocabulary serves the same function it did as separate skills, but without prescribing the order or completeness of application.
Alternatives: Reference mura/muri/muda directories as supplementary reading (creates dependency without benefit), include extended examples (violates P1 - defines the route instead of the destination)
### DEC-016: Repair this as a patch release (`v2.0.1`), not a rewrite or history edit.
2026-04-19T18:07:08+02:00 - [skills-v2-cross-validation](sessions/2026-04-19-skills-v2-cross-validation.md#L33)
Rationale: The rebuild itself was real and should remain in the trail. The defect is release integrity: the wrong live artifact was shipped. A patch release preserves the historical rebuild while correcting the shipped state.
Alternatives: rewrite the v2.0.0 trail as if the defect never happened (dishonest), invalidate the rebuild entirely (too strong - the design reasoning still stands)
### DEC-017: Remove the retired standalone skills from the live suite completely and prese...
2026-04-19T18:07:08+02:00 - [skills-v2-cross-validation](sessions/2026-04-19-skills-v2-cross-validation.md#L37)
Rationale: Leaving `mura`, `muri`, `muda`, and `project-increment` live in the suite root contradicts the core rebuild decision that the live suite is 5 skills. Reference material belongs in the archive, not beside active skills.
Alternatives: leave the directories in place with warning text (still exposes contradictory live artifacts), keep them as empty stubs (same contradiction)
### DEC-018: Verifier and ledgers must align to the 5-skill suite before any Tier 2 claim ...
2026-04-19T18:07:08+02:00 - [skills-v2-cross-validation](sessions/2026-04-19-skills-v2-cross-validation.md#L41)
Rationale: Transferability and observer satisfaction are only meaningful if the shipped artifact, the verifier, and the trail all describe the same system.
Alternatives: defer infrastructure alignment to a later run (would leave the suite mechanically dishonest)
### DEC-019: Absorb 3 gaps into existing skills rather than add new phases.
2026-04-20T17:08:47+02:00 - [design-discussion-metrics-phases](sessions/2026-04-20-design-discussion-metrics-phases.md#L51)
Rationale: 6 of 9 already covered; 3 genuine gaps are natural extensions of Kata Phase 1 (assumptions/constraints) and Hansei (drift/retirement). Adding phases contradicts the user's explicit constraint ("don't build a monster").
Alternatives: (a) add all 9 as new phases — rejected, creates bloat; (b) add 2-3 as new skills — rejected, contradicts v2 consolidation; (c) ignore all — rejected, 3 gaps are real.
### DEC-020: GENBA archival deferred to separate session.
2026-04-20T17:08:47+02:00 - [design-discussion-metrics-phases](sessions/2026-04-20-design-discussion-metrics-phases.md#L69)
Rationale: Archival is an infrastructure change that needs its own design (what to keep active, format of archive, how GENBA references work across archive boundary). This session's scope was the design discussion + approved lightweight changes.
Alternatives: *not recorded*
### DEC-021: Establish the metric-vs-vocabulary line: "If it can be counted, it's a metric...
2026-04-20T17:08:47+02:00 - [design-discussion-metrics-phases](sessions/2026-04-20-design-discussion-metrics-phases.md#L81)
Rationale: Metrics trigger the existing Kaizen diagnostic cycle without prescribing solutions (P1-compliant). Vocabulary provides thinking frameworks for situated reasoning that can't be reduced to a number. Future additions should be tested against this line.
Alternatives: (a) code efficiency tactics directly into skills — rejected, violates P1; (b) make everything a metric, including drift detection — rejected, "has intent drifted?" can't be meaningfully quantified without losing semantic content.
### DEC-022: Add one line to Kata Phase 1 (Grasp) making operational metrics legitimate im...
2026-04-20T17:08:47+02:00 - [design-discussion-metrics-phases](sessions/2026-04-20-design-discussion-metrics-phases.md#L85)
Rationale: The system already measures efficiency (Metrics 8-10) and already knows how to improve things that score POOR (Kaizen). The missing link was that nothing told the loop its own metrics are in scope. One line bridges that gap without prescribing solutions.
Alternatives: *not recorded*
### DEC-023: Run Hansei across both external runs rather than continuing to more targets.
2026-04-20T05:41:00+02:00 - [external-runs-and-restructuring](sessions/2026-04-20-external-runs-and-restructuring.md#L36)
Rationale: Two runs provide enough signal to reflect on the process. More runs without reflection risks repeating mistakes.
Alternatives: Run more external targets first (rejected — diminishing returns without examining what happened).
### DEC-024: Move GENBA.md into TRAIL/ directory as the canonical evidence location.
2026-04-20T05:41:00+02:00 - [external-runs-and-restructuring](sessions/2026-04-20-external-runs-and-restructuring.md#L51)
Rationale: GENBA is evidence, not a project-level artifact. Having evidence in two places (root and TRAIL/) forces observers to check both.
Alternatives: Keep at root (rejected — inconsistent with TRAIL/ being the single evidence location).
### DEC-025: Separate spec from implementation in PRINCIPLES.md.
2026-04-20T05:41:00+02:00 - [external-runs-and-restructuring](sessions/2026-04-20-external-runs-and-restructuring.md#L56)
Rationale: PRINCIPLES.md defined what Observable Autonomy requires. It should not name specific tools (GENBA.md, kiroku). An implementation could use databases, dashboards, or structured logs.
Alternatives: Keep the implementation references (rejected — makes the principles less portable, couples spec to tooling).
### DEC-026: Add decision quality enforcement to kiroku-validate.
2026-04-20T05:41:00+02:00 - [external-runs-and-restructuring](sessions/2026-04-20-external-runs-and-restructuring.md#L61)
Rationale: Finding 3 showed that decision rationale gets skipped under pressure. Validation should warn when this happens, creating friction against low-quality recording.
Alternatives: Rely on agent discipline alone (rejected — demonstrated to fail under speed pressure).
### DEC-027: Add observer entry point (TRAIL/README.md) with glossary and reading guide.
2026-04-20T05:41:00+02:00 - [external-runs-and-restructuring](sessions/2026-04-20-external-runs-and-restructuring.md#L66)
Rationale: Finding 4 showed undefined terms, uncalibrated fidelity, no reading order. An observer who doesn't already know the system cannot use the trail.
Alternatives: Add glossary to SUMMARY.md (rejected — SUMMARY changes every run; README is stable).
### DEC-028: Make kiroku a skill (with SKILL.md) rather than standalone tooling.
2026-04-20T05:41:00+02:00 - [external-runs-and-restructuring](sessions/2026-04-20-external-runs-and-restructuring.md#L77)
Rationale: Kiroku has a "when" (every session that produces decisions) and a "how" (the 4 scripts). Making it a skill means the agent is told to load it and follow its protocol whenever it does substantive work — including design conversations like this one. As standalone tooling, it would only be invoked when someone remembers to call the scripts.
Alternatives: Keep as tooling without SKILL.md (rejected — no trigger mechanism, relies on human memory to invoke it). Move into skills repo without SKILL.md as infrastructure (considered — but then the agent wouldn't know when/how to use it).
### DEC-029: Move kiroku from C:\git\kiroku\ into C:\Users\admin\.copilot\skills\kiroku\.
2026-04-20T05:41:00+02:00 - [external-runs-and-restructuring](sessions/2026-04-20-external-runs-and-restructuring.md#L83)
Rationale: Eliminates the kiroku repo as a separate artifact. The scripts belong with the skills they serve. One repo, one commit history.
Alternatives: Keep as separate repo (rejected — unevenness identified in Hansei).
### DEC-030: Split point: keep Runs 51-65 (15 entries) in active GENBA.md, archive Runs 1-...
2026-04-20T17:41:49+02:00 - [genba-archival](sessions/2026-04-20-genba-archival.md#L33)
Rationale: 15 entries provides sufficient recent history for Kata Phase 1 context without the cost of reading 178 KB. The ~15 target from the request aligns cleanly with the Run 51 boundary.
Alternatives: Keep 10 (too few context), keep 20 (still 80+ KB active).
### DEC-031: Metric 10 assesses active GENBA size only; archive size is reported but not s...
2026-04-20T17:41:49+02:00 - [genba-archival](sessions/2026-04-20-genba-archival.md#L37)
Rationale: The operational cost is reading the active file per run. Archive is read-once for verification, not per-run. Scoring total size would make the metric permanently POOR regardless of archival.
Alternatives: Score total size (rejected — defeats the purpose of archival).
### DEC-032: verify-suite.ps1 Checks 5, 9, 12 use combined active+archive content via `$gF...
2026-04-20T17:41:49+02:00 - [genba-archival](sessions/2026-04-20-genba-archival.md#L41)
Rationale: Coverage and consistency checks need full history. Latest-run identity check only needs the most recent entry.
Alternatives: *not recorded*
### DEC-033: Restructure kiroku/SKILL.md to principle-first order: Why → What → How → Refe...
2026-04-20T10:13:27+02:00 - [kaizen-kiroku-rewrite](sessions/2026-04-20-kaizen-kiroku-rewrite.md#L57)
Rationale: The current order (scripts → structure → concepts) assumes the reader already understands P2. A colleague encountering this for the first time needs the reasoning chain first: what problem does this solve → what does good evidence look like → how do I produce it → script reference.
Alternatives: Add a "Philosophy" section at the top while keeping existing structure (rejected — half-measure, still implementation-heavy). Create a separate human-readable guide (rejected — splits the canonical document, creates maintenance burden).
### DEC-034: Add root README.md and archive 7 journey documents to v1_archive/
2026-04-20T11:10:16+02:00 - [kaizen-suite-r50](sessions/2026-04-20-kaizen-suite-r50.md#L45)
Rationale: Directly serves Target Condition — "readable by someone with no prior context." A newcomer needs an entry point, not rebuild history.
Alternatives: (1) Leave as-is — rejected, violates Target Condition. (2) Delete journey docs — rejected, they have historical value. (3) Archive to v1_archive/ — chosen, preserves history while decluttering.
### DEC-035: Fix all 4 consistency gaps as Kaizen — no structural change needed
2026-04-20T10:32:39+02:00 - [kata-kiroku-consistency](sessions/2026-04-20-kata-kiroku-consistency.md#L42)
Rationale: All findings are leftovers from the GENBA separation and earlier Kata-centric framing. The structure is sound; these are just missed spots.
Alternatives: Leave README template as-is and fix only SKILL.md (rejected — new projects initialized by kiroku would get stale README). Rewrite scripts entirely (rejected — overreaction to consistency gaps).
### DEC-036: Measurement scheme for this target (TPS Skill Suite):
2026-04-20T11:43:30+02:00 - [kata-run-51-scored](sessions/2026-04-20-kata-run-51-scored.md#L43)

Alternatives: Context-only measurements without Rubric v3 (rejected — the formal rubric exists for this target and the protocol says "if a formal rubric exists, use it").

### DEC-044: Make target-repo routing explicit in the global VS Code instruction, `kata/SK...

### DEC-045: Migrate the skills suite's live run ledger from repo root `GENBA.md` to `TRAI...

Rationale: Self-targeting should follow the same contract as every other target repo. The skills suite cannot prescribe `TARGET_REPO/TRAIL/` to others while keeping its own live ledger outside `TRAIL/`.
Alternatives: Keep root `GENBA.md` for the skills suite as a special case (rejected — special-casing the suite breaks the rule the user wants developers to rely on).
### DEC-046: Update `verify-suite.ps1` and `metrics.ps1` to use `TRAIL/GENBA.md` as the li...
2026-04-20T06:08:50+02:00 - [kata-self-target-gpt54-xhigh](sessions/2026-04-20-kata-self-target-gpt54-xhigh.md#L37)
Rationale: Leaving the scripts on the old root path would make the migration superficial and cause the suite to fail its own mechanical checks.
Alternatives: Add fallback logic for both root and `TRAIL/` locations (rejected — that preserves ambiguity instead of enforcing one canonical location).
### DEC-047: Start Kiroku immediately once the lapse was noticed and record the late start...

Rationale: Continuing the evaluation without a session transcript would deepen the Principle 2 violation. Pretending Kiroku had been running from the start would be dishonest.

### DEC-048: Explain the 3M change as an intentional redesign choice, not as accidental de...
2026-04-20T16:01:34+02:00 - [kata-tps-fresh-eval-gpt54](sessions/2026-04-20-kata-tps-fresh-eval-gpt54.md#L65)
Rationale: the live artifacts show that v2 collapsed the standalone 3M workflows into Kaizen's diagnostic vocabulary. The concepts survived; only the packaging changed.
Alternatives: describe them as removed outright (rejected - misleading, because Kaizen still uses unevenness/overburden/waste lenses), describe them as still-live skills (rejected - false, because the runnable live suite no longer includes those directories).
### DEC-049: Fix `verify-suite.ps1` Check 5 at the inclusion-rule level instead of editing...

Rationale: the ledgers were internally consistent with the suite's actual behavior. The defect was the verifier's stale assumption that all `*external*` rows should be excluded from suite-GENBA accounting.

### DEC-050: Record the residual Kiroku warning honestly rather than retroactively inventi...

Alternatives: patch the old sessions with invented alternatives (rejected - dishonest reconstruction), broaden the validator to ignore `*not recorded*` (rejected - hides real historical evidence gaps).
### DEC-051: Tighten `kiroku-validate.ps1` Check 7 to count only actual rationale/alternat...
2026-04-20T16:01:34+02:00 - [kata-tps-fresh-eval-gpt54](sessions/2026-04-20-kata-tps-fresh-eval-gpt54.md#L98)
Rationale: the validator should detect missing evidence fields, not arbitrary narrative references to those words.
Alternatives: avoid mentioning the phrase in narrative text (rejected - brittle and backwards; the validator should parse the structure it claims to validate), accept the inflated warning count (rejected - false positive normalizes a tool defect).
### DEC-052: Move GENBA.md ownership from kiroku to Kata
2026-04-20T10:29:08+02:00 - [kiroku-genba-separation](sessions/2026-04-20-kiroku-genba-separation.md#L38)
Rationale: GENBA contains methodology-specific fields (run number, 3M diagnosis, methodology selection) that only make sense in Kata's improvement context. A standalone kiroku trail for non-software work (legal review, hiring, design sprint) would never produce a GENBA. Kiroku's job is evidence capture (sessions + decisions + summary). Kata's job is improvement methodology — GENBA is its run ledger.
Alternatives: Keep GENBA in kiroku but make it generic (rejected — a generic "run ledger" is just a worse SUMMARY.md). Remove GENBA entirely (rejected — it serves Kata well, just doesn't belong to kiroku).
### DEC-053: Make all measurements context-derived, not universal
2026-04-20T11:35:52+02:00 - [measurement-protocol](sessions/2026-04-20-measurement-protocol.md#L33)
Rationale: The suite must work on any repo. A skill suite needs ARF/P1/P2 measurement. A driving school website needs security/accessibility/UX measurement. Prescribing universal metrics violates P1 (Commander's Intent). The agent derives what to measure after Grasp, based on the target's nature and Target Condition.
Alternatives: (1) Three fixed tiers (universal + standards + context) — rejected: even "universal" tier would contain things irrelevant to some targets. (2) No measurement at all — rejected: this is exactly the v2 gap that produced 6 consecutive N/A runs.
### DEC-054: Agent inherits prior run's measurement scheme by default
2026-04-20T11:35:52+02:00 - [measurement-protocol](sessions/2026-04-20-measurement-protocol.md#L37)
Rationale: Measurement continuity enables gap tracking across runs. If every run derives fresh measurements, scores become incomparable. Revision is allowed but must be recorded as a [!DECISION] so observers can see why the basis changed.
Alternatives: *not recorded*
### DEC-055: v2.4.0 (minor), not v2.3.1 (patch).
2026-04-20T18:13:28+02:00 - [release-v2-4-0](sessions/2026-04-20-release-v2-4-0.md#L33)
Rationale: Metric 11 adds a new observable (reviewer engagement), the evidence-substrate reframe narrows the framework's trust claim, and the Out-of-Scope section makes eight scope boundaries explicit. These are material changes to what the framework says it does, not bug fixes. Keep-a-Changelog convention: new features = minor bump.
Alternatives: v2.3.1 (patch) rejected as undersell; v3.0.0 (major) rejected because the principles are unchanged — this is narrowing, not redesign.
### DEC-056: Consolidate all Unreleased items (Runs 59-65) into 2.4.0 rather than splittin...
2026-04-20T18:13:28+02:00 - [release-v2-4-0](sessions/2026-04-20-release-v2-4-0.md#L37)
Rationale: The Unreleased items accumulated because no release boundary was drawn between Run 59 and now. A single consolidated 2.4.0 is honest about what actually shipped and when. Splitting retroactively would invent release boundaries that didn't exist.
Alternatives: tag each run separately (rejected — inaccurate rewrite of history).
### DEC-057: ` matching to line start (`^\s*\[!DECISION\]`) in both kiroku-index.ps1 and k...
2026-04-20T10:56:34+02:00 - [suite-tightening](sessions/2026-04-20-suite-tightening.md#L35)
Rationale: Real decision markers always start the line. False positives occur when the marker text appears mid-sentence in descriptions or references. Anchoring to line start eliminates false positives without losing real matches.
Alternatives: Backtick detection (tried first — didn't work because the false positives weren't backtick-quoted). More complex heuristics (unnecessary — line-start anchor is sufficient and simple).
### DEC-058: Frame the suite as an "evidence substrate" rather than a "trust model" in PRI...
2026-04-20T17:53:24+02:00 - [trust-substrate-reframe](sessions/2026-04-20-trust-substrate-reframe.md#L39)
Rationale: The critique's most precise point (item 7) was that what was built is governance + evidence, not trust itself. Trust requires shared values, aligned incentives, demonstrated reliability over time — only the third is in scope. Calling it a trust model overclaims; calling it an evidence substrate is honest about what it produces and what it depends on others to produce.
Alternatives: *not recorded*
### DEC-059: Reviewer-engagement mechanism: a checkbox + date + reviewer field at the top ...
2026-04-20T17:53:24+02:00 - [trust-substrate-reframe](sessions/2026-04-20-trust-substrate-reframe.md#L43)
Rationale: The user's proposal is the smallest possible mechanism that makes review activity *observable*. It does not solve the reviewer-engagement problem, but it makes non-engagement visible — which is the framework's only legitimate move under its own principles. A green Metric 11 means review happened; a red Metric 11 means the trail is accumulating unread, and the operator can act on that signal.
Alternatives: *not recorded*
### DEC-060: Add Out-of-Scope section to PROBLEM.md naming all eight critique items as sco...
2026-04-20T17:53:24+02:00 - [trust-substrate-reframe](sessions/2026-04-20-trust-substrate-reframe.md#L47)
Rationale: Half of the defensive answers I would otherwise re-derive every time the critique is raised are "that's not what this is for." Writing it down once is cheaper, more honest, and itself an instance of Observable Autonomy applied to the framework's own design.
Alternatives: *not recorded*
### DEC-061: Prioritize two script changes only: start-time overlap prevention and validat...
2026-04-21T03:47:48+02:00 - [kata-p2-observability-roi](sessions/2026-04-21-kata-p2-observability-roi.md#L42)
Rationale: These changes directly target missing/stale evidence risk with minimal runtime and implementation cost, matching the ROI mandate.
Alternatives: broader redesign of Kiroku templates/process rejected as unnecessary workflow drag.
### DEC-062: Replace low-signal historical warning noise with checks that detect silent ga...
2026-04-21T03:47:48+02:00 - [kata-p2-observability-roi](sessions/2026-04-21-kata-p2-observability-roi.md#L46)
Rationale: Warning on old `*not recorded*` fields is explicit legacy debt, not a silent gap. Freshness, active-session completeness, scope correctness, and secret hygiene checks reduce present trust risk more.
Alternatives: backfilling historical rationale/alternatives rejected due fidelity-honesty violation risk.
### DEC-063: Stop after current change set.
2026-04-21T03:47:48+02:00 - [kata-p2-observability-roi](sessions/2026-04-21-kata-p2-observability-roi.md#L62)
Rationale: all stated constraints are met with clean verification, and further additions would increase complexity more than trust value.
Alternatives: add more policy checks or template redesign rejected as lower ROI and higher friction.
### DEC-064: Add SCORECARD row for Run 70. Root cause: Run 70 executor labeled run "non-sc...
2026-04-21T05:40:09+02:00 - [kata-self-p3](sessions/2026-04-21-kata-self-p3.md#L84)
Rationale: *not recorded*
Alternatives: (a) accept the gap — rejected, verifier failing is not acceptable background noise; (b) change the convention so non-scoring runs skip SCORECARD — rejected, the existing convention is correct and consistent with all prior non-scoring rows. P3 does not advance because this is an artifact change
### DEC-065: Recommend a canonical step spine for all runs, with mandatory per-step output...
2026-04-21T04:32:41+02:00 - [kata-standardize-framework-steps](sessions/2026-04-21-kata-standardize-framework-steps.md#L40)
Rationale: This keeps process reconstructable and comparable while admitting human-context differences that AI cannot infer perfectly.
Alternatives: Fully custom flow per audience rejected (too much variance and weaker auditability); fully rigid one-size-fits-all narrative rejected (poor fit for human stakeholders).
### DEC-066: Keep this record as reconstructed evidence and close it explicitly rather tha...
2026-04-21T03:03:30+02:00 - [run-67-trail-housekeeping](sessions/2026-04-21-run-67-trail-housekeeping.md#L40)
Rationale: explicit closure preserves auditability and avoids silent trail gaps.
Alternatives: deleting the session rejected (loss of evidence continuity).
### DEC-067: Selected Kaizen's Challenge Blind Spots claim as the probe target because: (1...
2026-04-21T05:34:13+02:00 - [shiken-run66](sessions/2026-04-21-shiken-run66.md#L33)
Rationale: *not recorded*
Alternatives: *not recorded*
